nextToken.description: Next token returned in the response of a previous ListConfigs call. Used to get the next page of results.
Next token returned in the response of a previous ListDataflowEndpointGroups call. Used to get the next page of results.
Next token that can be supplied in the next call to get the next page of ground stations.
Next token returned in the response of a previous ListMissionProfiles call. Used to get the next page of results.
Next token that can be supplied in the next call to get the next page of satellites.

groundStations.description: A list of ground stations to which the satellite is on-boarded.

currentEphemeris.description: The current ephemeris being used to compute the trajectory of the satellite.

ephemerisId.description:
UUID of a customer-provided ephemeris.

This field is not populated for default ephemerides from Space Track.

epoch.description:
The epoch of a default, ephemeris from Space Track in UTC.

This field is not populated for customer-provided ephemerides.

name.description:
A name string associated with the ephemeris. Used as a human-readable identifier for the ephemeris.

A name is only returned for customer-provider ephemerides that have a name associated.

startTime.description: Start time of a contact in UTC.

endTime.description: End time of a contact in UTC.

prePassStartTime.description: Amount of time prior to contact start you’d like to receive a CloudWatch event indicating an upcoming pass.

postPassEndTime.description: Amount of time after a contact ends that you’d like to receive a CloudWatch event indicating the pass has finished.

dataflowList.description: List describing source and destination details for each dataflow edge.

visibilityStartTime.description: Projected time in UTC your satellite will rise above the receive mask. This time is based on the satellite's current active ephemeris for future contacts and the ephemeris that was active during contact execution for completed contacts.

visibilityEndTime.description: Projected time in UTC your satellite will set below the receive mask. This time is based on the satellite's current active ephemeris for future contacts and the ephemeris that was active during contact execution for completed contacts.

trackingOverrides.description: Tracking configuration overrides specified when the contact was reserved.

ephemeris.description: The ephemeris that determines antenna pointing directions for the contact.

configDetails.description: Additional details for a Config, if type is dataflow-endpoint or antenna-downlink-demod-decode

securityDetails.description: Endpoint security details including a list of subnets, a list of security groups and a role to connect streams to instances.

healthStatus.description: A dataflow endpoint health status. This field is ignored when calling CreateDataflowEndpointGroup.

healthReasons.description: Health reasons for a dataflow endpoint. This field is ignored when calling CreateDataflowEndpointGroup.

antennaDemodDecodeDetails.description: Details for antenna demod decode Config in a contact.

outputNode.description: Name of an antenna demod decode output node used in a contact.

s3RecordingDetails.description: Details for an S3 recording Config in a contact.

keyTemplate.description: Key template used for the S3 Recording Configuration

configDetails.description: Additional details for a Config, if type is dataflow endpoint or antenna demod decode.

securityDetails.description: Endpoint security details including a list of subnets, a list of security groups and a role to connect streams to instances.

healthStatus.description: A dataflow endpoint health status. This field is ignored when calling CreateDataflowEndpointGroup.

healthReasons.description: Health reasons for a dataflow endpoint. This field is ignored when calling CreateDataflowEndpointGroup.

antennaDemodDecodeDetails.description: Details for antenna demod decode Config in a contact.

outputNode.description: Name of an antenna demod decode output node used in a contact.

s3RecordingDetails.description: Details for an S3 recording Config in a contact.

keyTemplate.description: Key template used for the S3 Recording Configuration

satelliteId.description: The AWS Ground Station satellite ID associated with ephemeris.

priority.description:
A priority score that determines which ephemeris to use when multiple ephemerides overlap.

Higher numbers take precedence. The default is 1. Must be 1 or greater.

creationTime.description: The time the ephemeris was uploaded in UTC.

enabled.description: Whether or not the ephemeris is enabled.

name.description: A name that you can use to identify the ephemeris.

invalidReason.description: Reason that an ephemeris failed validation. Appears only when the status is INVALID.

errorReasons.description:
Detailed error information for ephemerides with INVALID status.

Provides specific error codes and messages to help diagnose validation failures.

errorCode.description:
The error code identifying the type of validation failure.

See the Troubleshooting Invalid Ephemerides guide for error code details.

errorMessage.description:
A human-readable message describing the validation failure.

Provides specific details about what failed and may include suggestions for remediation.

presignedLogUrl.description: Presigned URL for uploading agent task response logs.

antennaDownlinkConfig.description: Information about how AWS Ground Station should configure an antenna for downlink during a contact.

centerFrequency.description: Center frequency of a spectral Config. Valid values are between 2200 to 2300 MHz and 7750 to 8400 MHz for downlink and 2025 to 2120 MHz for uplink.

value.description: Frequency value. Valid values are between 2200 to 2300 MHz and 7750 to 8400 MHz for downlink and 2025 to 2120 MHz for uplink.

bandwidth.description:
Bandwidth of a spectral Config. AWS Ground Station currently has the following bandwidth limitations:

  • For AntennaDownlinkDemodDecodeconfig, valid values are between 125 kHz to 650 MHz.

  • For AntennaDownlinkconfig valid values are between 10 kHz to 54 MHz.

  • For AntennaUplinkConfig, valid values are between 10 kHz to 54 MHz.

value.description:
Frequency bandwidth value. AWS Ground Station currently has the following bandwidth limitations:

  • For AntennaDownlinkDemodDecodeconfig, valid values are between 125 kHz to 650 MHz.

  • For AntennaDownlinkconfig, valid values are between 10 kHz to 54 MHz.

  • For AntennaUplinkConfig, valid values are between 10 kHz to 54 MHz.

polarization.description: Polarization of a spectral Config. Capturing both "RIGHT_HAND" and "LEFT_HAND" polarization requires two separate configs.

trackingConfig.description: Object that determines whether tracking should be used during a contact executed with this Config in the mission profile.

antennaDownlinkDemodDecodeConfig.description: Information about how AWS Ground Station should conﬁgure an antenna for downlink demod decode during a contact.

centerFrequency.description: Center frequency of a spectral Config. Valid values are between 2200 to 2300 MHz and 7750 to 8400 MHz for downlink and 2025 to 2120 MHz for uplink.

value.description: Frequency value. Valid values are between 2200 to 2300 MHz and 7750 to 8400 MHz for downlink and 2025 to 2120 MHz for uplink.

bandwidth.description:
Bandwidth of a spectral Config. AWS Ground Station currently has the following bandwidth limitations:

  • For AntennaDownlinkDemodDecodeconfig, valid values are between 125 kHz to 650 MHz.

  • For AntennaDownlinkconfig valid values are between 10 kHz to 54 MHz.

  • For AntennaUplinkConfig, valid values are between 10 kHz to 54 MHz.

value.description:
Frequency bandwidth value. AWS Ground Station currently has the following bandwidth limitations:

  • For AntennaDownlinkDemodDecodeconfig, valid values are between 125 kHz to 650 MHz.

  • For AntennaDownlinkconfig, valid values are between 10 kHz to 54 MHz.

  • For AntennaUplinkConfig, valid values are between 10 kHz to 54 MHz.

polarization.description: Polarization of a spectral Config. Capturing both "RIGHT_HAND" and "LEFT_HAND" polarization requires two separate configs.

antennaUplinkConfig.description: Information about how AWS Ground Station should conﬁgure an antenna for uplink during a contact.

transmitDisabled.description: Whether or not uplink transmit is disabled.

centerFrequency.description: Center frequency of an uplink spectral Config. Valid values are between 2025 to 2120 MHz.

value.description: Frequency value. Valid values are between 2200 to 2300 MHz and 7750 to 8400 MHz for downlink and 2025 to 2120 MHz for uplink.

polarization.description: Polarization of an uplink spectral Config. Capturing both "RIGHT_HAND" and "LEFT_HAND" polarization requires two separate configs.

value.description: Value of an EIRP. Valid values are between 20.0 to 50.0 dBW.

uplinkEchoConfig.description:
Information about an uplink echo Config.

Parameters from the AntennaUplinkConfig, corresponding to the specified AntennaUplinkConfigArn, are used when this UplinkEchoConfig is used in a contact.

enabled.description: Whether or not an uplink Config is enabled.

bucketArn.description: ARN of the bucket to record to.

roleArn.description: ARN of the role Ground Station assumes to write data to the bucket.

prefix.description: S3 Key prefix to prefice data files.

tags.description: Tags assigned to a dataflow endpoint group.

contactPrePassDurationSeconds.description: Amount of time, in seconds, before a contact starts that the Ground Station Dataflow Endpoint Group will be in a PREPASS state. A Ground Station Dataflow Endpoint Group State Change event will be emitted when the Dataflow Endpoint Group enters and exits the PREPASS state.

contactPostPassDurationSeconds.description: Amount of time, in seconds, after a contact ends that the Ground Station Dataflow Endpoint Group will be in a POSTPASS state. A Ground Station Dataflow Endpoint Group State Change event will be emitted when the Dataflow Endpoint Group enters and exits the POSTPASS state.

securityDetails.description: Endpoint security details including a list of subnets, a list of security groups and a role to connect streams to instances.

subnetIds.description: A list of subnets where AWS Ground Station places elastic network interfaces to send streams to your instances.

securityGroupIds.description: The security groups to attach to the elastic network interfaces.

roleArn.description: ARN to a role needed for connecting streams to your instances.

mtu.description: Maximum transmission unit (MTU) size in bytes of a dataflow endpoint.

name.description: Name string associated with AgentEndpoint. Used as a human-readable identifier for AgentEndpoint.

mtu.description: Maximum transmission unit (MTU) size in bytes of a dataflow endpoint.
Maximum transmission unit (MTU) size in bytes of a dataflow endpoint.

agentConnectionDetails.description: Uplink connection details for customer to Agent and Agent to Ground Station

ingressAddressAndPort.description: Egress address of AgentEndpoint with an optional mtu.

agentIpAndPortAddress.description: Ingress address of AgentEndpoint with a port range and an optional mtu.

agentStatus.description: Status of the agent associated with the uplink dataflow endpoint

auditResults.description: Health audit results for the uplink dataflow endpoint

agentConnectionDetails.description: Downlink connection details for customer to Agent and Agent to Ground Station

agentIpAndPortAddress.description: Ingress address of AgentEndpoint with a port range and an optional mtu.

egressAddressAndPort.description: Egress address of AgentEndpoint with an optional mtu.

agentStatus.description: Status of the agent associated with the downlink dataflow endpoint

auditResults.description: Health audit results for the downlink dataflow endpoint

healthStatus.description: A dataflow endpoint health status. This field is ignored when calling CreateDataflowEndpointGroup.

healthReasons.description: Health reasons for a dataflow endpoint. This field is ignored when calling CreateDataflowEndpointGroup.

isReservedMinutesCustomer.description: Returns whether or not an account has signed up for the reserved minutes pricing plan, specific to the month being requested.

totalReservedMinuteAllocation.description: Total number of reserved minutes allocated, specific to the month being requested.

upcomingMinutesScheduled.description: Upcoming minutes scheduled for an account, specific to the month being requested.

totalScheduledMinutes.description: Total scheduled minutes for an account, specific to the month being requested.

estimatedMinutesRemaining.description: Estimated number of minutes remaining for an account, specific to the month being requested.

contactPrePassDurationSeconds.description: Amount of time prior to contact start you’d like to receive a CloudWatch event indicating an upcoming pass.

contactPostPassDurationSeconds.description: Amount of time after a contact ends that you’d like to receive a CloudWatch event indicating the pass has finished.

minimumViableContactDurationSeconds.description: Smallest amount of time in seconds that you’d like to see for an available contact. AWS Ground Station will not present you with contacts shorter than this duration.

dataflowEdges.description: A list of lists of ARNs. Each list of ARNs is an edge, with a from Config and a to Config.

streamsKmsKey.description: KMS key to use for encrypting streams.

streamsKmsRole.description: Role to use for encrypting streams with KMS key.

groundStations.description: A list of ground stations to which the satellite is on-boarded.

currentEphemeris.description: The current ephemeris being used to compute the trajectory of the satellite.

nextToken.description: Next token returned in the response of a previous ListContacts call. Used to get the next page of results.

startTime.description: Start time of a contact in UTC.

endTime.description: End time of a contact in UTC.

prePassStartTime.description: Amount of time prior to contact start you’d like to receive a CloudWatch event indicating an upcoming pass.

postPassEndTime.description: Amount of time after a contact ends that you’d like to receive a CloudWatch event indicating the pass has finished.

visibilityStartTime.description: Projected time in UTC your satellite will rise above the receive mask. This time is based on the satellite's current active ephemeris for future contacts and the ephemeris that was active during contact execution for completed contacts. This field is not present for contacts with a SCHEDULING or SCHEDULED status.

visibilityEndTime.description: Projected time in UTC your satellite will set below the receive mask. This time is based on the satellite's current active ephemeris for future contacts and the ephemeris that was active during contact execution for completed contacts. This field is not present for contacts with a SCHEDULING or SCHEDULED status.

ephemeris.description: The ephemeris that determines antenna pointing for the contact.

ephemerisId.description: Unique identifier of the ephemeris. Appears only for custom ephemerides.

priority.description:
A priority score that determines which ephemeris to use when multiple ephemerides overlap.

Higher numbers take precedence. The default is 1. Must be 1 or greater.

enabled.description: Whether or not the ephemeris is enabled.

creationTime.description: The time the ephemeris was uploaded in UTC.

name.description: A name that you can use to identify the ephemeris.

sourceS3Object.description: Source Amazon S3 object used for the ephemeris.

key.description: An Amazon S3 key for the ephemeris.

version.description: For versioned Amazon S3 objects, the version to use for the ephemeris.
